generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controlled heating element using resistive heating with adjustable power output, limited by material constraints and energy efficiency considerations. Implements discrete on/off control logic (Bang-Bang) based on DT-generated insights."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical cooling component with variable speed control for dissipating excess heat. Constrained by mechanical tolerances and noise levels, operates under supervisory control from the Digital Twin."
]

instance EmbeddedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi-based embedded system interfacing with actuators (HeatbedActuator, FanActuator) to execute control commands derived from Digital Twin analysis. Communicates via serial/USB protocols."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer measuring localized thermal conditions within the styrofoam enclosure, with ±0.5°C accuracy across -10°C to +85°C range."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer measuring heatbed surface temperature within the styrofoam enclosure, with ±0.5°C accuracy across -10°C to +85°C range."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData2
]

instance AmbientTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer positioned outside the styrofoam enclosure to capture ambient environmental conditions, with ±0.5°C accuracy across -10°C to +85°C range."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AmbientTemperatureData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance InternalTemperatureData1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
]

instance InternalTemperatureData2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance AmbientTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AmbientTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ThermalBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based differential equation model simulating heat transfer dynamics within the styrofoam enclosure, calibrated using real-time temperature data from InternalTemperatureSensor1 and InternalTemperatureSensor2."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQBroker
]

instance EnvironmentalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model predicting ambient temperature impacts on system performance, incorporating AmbientTemperatureData for external condition analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQBroker
]

instance SensedTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated real-time temperature measurements from all DS18S20 sensors (InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, AmbientTemperatureSensor) transmitted via RabbitMQ for model calibration."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureData1, InternalTemperatureData2, AmbientTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm
]

instance OperationalPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical and real-time performance metrics derived from SensedTemperatureData, used for fault detection and optimization analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SensedTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MonitoringAlgorithm
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RabbitMQBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ message broker facilitating communication between physical sensors and Digital Twin components, ensuring reliable data transmission via 1-Wire protocol integration."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataIngestionService, ModelCalibrationService
]

instance CalibrationAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python-based parameter estimation algorithm using least squares optimization to dynamically adjust ThermalBehaviorModel and EnvironmentalModel parameters based on SensedTemperatureData."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCalibrationService
]

instance MonitoringAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "State estimator algorithm analyzing OperationalPerformanceData to detect deviations from expected behavior, triggering safety and optimality alerts."
    DTDFVocab:enables FaultDetectionService, PerformanceMonitoringService
]

instance ControlInterface : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi-based interface executing actuation commands (HeatbedActuator, FanActuator) derived from Digital Twin insights, implementing Bang-Bang control logic."
    DTDFVocab:enables ActuationService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataIngestionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service responsible for receiving and preprocessing DataTransmitted objects (InternalTemperatureData1, InternalTemperatureData2, AmbientTemperatureData) before model ingestion."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataValidationInsight
]

instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service dynamically recalibrating ThermalBehaviorModel and EnvironmentalModel using CalibrationAlgorithm, ensuring alignment between simulated and actual system states."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service identifying anomalies or failures in the Physical Twin based on MonitoringAlgorithm analysis of OperationalPerformanceData."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionInsight
]

instance PerformanceMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service continuously assessing system health and operational efficiency through real-time performance metrics analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceEfficiencyInsight
]

instance ActuationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing control commands to HeatbedActuator and FanActuator based on Digital Twin recommendations, implementing supervisory control logic."
    DTDFVocab:provides ActuationRecommendationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance DataValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation status of incoming temperature data from DS18S20 sensors, ensuring data quality before model ingestion."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Confidence measure of model predictions derived from calibration algorithm performance, indicating alignment between simulated and actual system behavior."
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified anomalies or failures in the Physical Twin system, including temperature deviations or actuator malfunctions."
]

instance PerformanceEfficiencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous assessment of system operational efficiency, highlighting deviations from optimal performance metrics."
]

instance ActuationRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated control adjustments for HeatbedActuator and FanActuator based on Digital Twin predictions, implementing Bang-Bang control logic."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance OverheatProtectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Emergency shutdown procedure for HeatbedActuator if temperature exceeds safe limits, implemented as a hardware safety constraint."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C12: Environments
instance StyrofoamEnclosureEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, HeatbedActuator, FanActuator
]

instance ExternalEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains AmbientTemperatureSensor
]

// C13: Components
instance DigitalThermalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Centralized Digital Twin instance integrating all models, data, and algorithms for real-time monitoring, calibration, and optimization of the Physical Twin system."
]

instance PhysicalThermalSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Cyber-physical system comprising styrofoam enclosure with embedded sensors (DS18S20) and actuators (HeatbedActuator, FanActuator) controlled via Raspberry Pi."
    base:isContainedIn StyrofoamEnclosureEnvironment, ExternalEnvironment
]

// Fixed DataFlow instances
instance DataIngestionFlow : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureData1, InternalTemperatureData2, AmbientTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIngestionService
]

instance ModelCalibrationFlow : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData SensedTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm
]

instance MonitoringFlow : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalPerformanceData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MonitoringAlgorithm
]

instance ActuationFlow : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuationRecommendationAction
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlInterface
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT system employs **calibration algorithms** and **monitoring systems** to detect deviations or violations in the PT’s performance, triggering corrective actions via a **bang-bang controller** for low-level adjustments (e.g., fan or heatbed control in an incubator system). Commands are transmitted from the DT to the PT through the same **RabbitMQ server**, adhering to a **safe protocol** for low-level driver interaction. Validation and authentication mechanisms are not explicitly detailed, but the system relies on the DT’s models and real-time data to ensure commands align with the PT’s operational constraints. Feedback from the PT is looped back into the DT for continuous optimization, with no explicit mention of conflict resolution or command execution confirmation mechanisms."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates with temporal synchronization between the Physical Twin (PT) and its digital counterpart through continuous or periodic data collection from sensors, as implied by the requirement to 'reproduce and predict the behaviour of the PT under its current operating conditions.' The document does not specify exact synchronization frequencies or latency requirements, but it highlights the necessity of real-time or near-real-time data acquisition to inform calibration algorithms and monitoring processes. The temporal granularity is dictated by the operational dynamics of the Physical Twin and the responsiveness of the system's algorithms (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators) to maintain accurate predictive and analytical capabilities. No explicit time scales (e.g., milliseconds, seconds, minutes) or quantitative latency constraints are provided in the document."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **modular and integrated architecture pattern**, combining **data collection** (from PT sensors), **modeling** (of CPS and environmental physics), and **algorithmic processing** (e.g., fault detection, state estimation, optimization) into a cohesive framework. The document outlines a **closed-loop structure** where calibration algorithms update models in real-time, feeding monitored data into **supervisory controllers** that assess system performance. Resource management is implicitly handled through the **dynamic allocation of computational resources** to prioritize critical services (e.g., safety monitoring over predictive simulations). System-wide coordination is achieved via **feedback loops** between the DT and PT, where detected violations trigger **human or automated interventions** to adjust the PT's operation. The orchestration relies on **interdependent components**—data, models, and algorithms—working in tandem to add value to the PT, with no explicit mention of cloud-edge-fog architectures or specific orchestration frameworks (e.g., Kubernetes, microservices)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) spans multiple phases, beginning with **model development**—where abstract representations (e.g., CAD models for geometry, dynamic models for physics, or cyber-physical system (CPS) models) are constructed to capture specific aspects of the Physical Twin (PT) such as structural properties, environmental interactions, or operational dynamics. These models are iteratively refined through **simulation and validation**, where differential equations or other mathematical frameworks (e.g., [3, Section 5]) are employed to analyze behavior under hypothetical conditions.

During the **deployment phase**, the DT integrates with the PT via **real-time data collection** from sensors, enabling continuous calibration of models using algorithms (e.g., parameter estimation techniques) to align simulated and actual system states. This phase also includes **monitoring and optimization**, where data-driven algorithms (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators, or supervisory controllers) analyze deviations from expected performance, triggering corrective actions via human intervention or automated feedback loops.

**Lifecycle governance** is implicitly addressed through the retention of models post-deployment, ensuring they are not discarded but instead **embedded into the DT framework** for ongoing analysis. Transition management between phases involves **iterative refinement cycles**, where insights from monitoring inform updates to models and algorithms, creating a closed-loop evolution. The lifecycle is governed by the interplay between **model fidelity** (balancing abstraction and detail) and **operational relevance**, ensuring the DT remains a dynamic, value-additive extension of the PT.

No explicit standards or frameworks for lifecycle governance are mentioned in the document, but the process is implicitly structured around iterative validation, real-time calibration, and adaptive optimization.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system incorporates **fidelity and validity considerations** to ensure models and data accurately represent the Physical Twin (PT). Key aspects include:

1. **Model Fidelity Requirements**:
   - **Assumption 3**: Explicit reliance on simplified thermal models (e.g., lumped capacitance approximations) to balance computational efficiency and accuracy.
   - **Dynamic Calibration**: Models are iteratively refined using **least squares optimization** to minimize discrepancies between predicted and observed behavior.

2. **Validation and Verification Methods**:
   - **Parameter Estimation**: Calibration algorithms adjust model parameters (e.g., thermal conductivities, heat capacities) based on real-time sensor data.
   - **Cross-Validation**: Predictive models are tested against historical PT data to verify consistency and robustness.

3. **Uncertainty Sources and Quantification**:
   - **Sensor Noise**: Measurement inaccuracies (±0.5°C) introduce uncertainty in model inputs.
   - **Model Simplifications**: Assumptions (e.g., Assumption 3) may introduce systematic errors in dynamic simulations.
   - **Environmental Variability**: External factors (e.g., ambient temperature fluctuations) affect PT behavior and model predictions.

4. **Error Detection and Correction Mechanisms**:
   - **Anomaly Detection**: Monitors flag deviations (e.g., temperature spikes) beyond calibrated error bounds, triggering recalibration or alerts.
   - **Automated Correction**: Algorithms (e.g., state estimators) adjust models to compensate for detected errors in real time.

5. **Quality Assurance Processes**:
   - **Periodic Recalibration**: Models are periodically updated (frequency not specified) to account for PT degradation or environmental changes.
   - **Human Oversight**: Critical validation steps may require expert review (implied by the need for human intervention in decision-making).
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for this DT system relies on a **Raspberry Pi 4 Model B** with built-in **Wi-Fi** for wireless communication, supporting out-of-the-box internet connectivity. The primary communication protocol is **RabbitMQ**, a message broker deployed locally on the Raspberry Pi to facilitate inter-process communication between the PT and DT components. Network topology is **single-node embedded**, with no mention of distributed or cloud-based architectures. Bandwidth and latency requirements are not specified, but the system’s simplicity suggests low overhead. **Reliability mechanisms** are not explicitly described beyond calibration algorithms, which refine model parameters but do not address transmission errors or network redundancy. Security protocols for network communication are **not mentioned** in the documents.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT system is deployed on a **Raspberry Pi 4 Model B**, with both the communication server and controller implemented locally on the same device. No cloud, edge, or on-premise hosting distinctions are made; the entire DT infrastructure operates as a **single-node embedded system**. Computational and storage requirements are minimal, limited by the Raspberry Pi’s hardware capabilities. Scalability mechanisms are not applicable, and deployment models are restricted to the local, standalone setup. Availability and reliability are not quantified, but the system’s simplicity suggests low fault tolerance without redundancy."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) system incorporates **safety monitoring** through embedded algorithms that continuously assess the operational performance of the Physical Twin (PT) by comparing real-time sensor data against calibrated models. When deviations or violations of predefined safety thresholds are detected (e.g., unsafe operational states or suboptimal performance), the system triggers alerts. These monitors leverage **calibration algorithms** to update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment with the best parameter estimates, ensuring the DT remains aligned with the PT’s actual state. While the document does not explicitly detail **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., encryption, firewalls, or intrusion detection systems), it implies a **low-level safety protocol** is integrated into the control logic to mitigate risks. However, no explicit mention of **access control mechanisms**, **authentication systems**, or **threat mitigation strategies** (e.g., adversarial manipulation defenses) is provided. The system relies on **human-in-the-loop** decision-making for corrective actions when violations are detected, but no formal **risk assessment framework** or **fail-safe mechanisms** (e.g., redundancy, graceful degradation) are described beyond the monitoring layer."
]
